last_commits: 0cb5e0a8c0b3 - tests: Increase test01 timeout and document kworker competition
fcb97c1f9373 - tests: Add journal logging for test activity correlation

current_status.solution: Increased timeout to 180s with comprehensive documentation

investigation_summary.original_problem: test01 failing with exit code 137 (SIGKILL from timeout) after 60s
investigation_summary.root_cause_identified: Timing variability from competing kworker starvation on loaded systems

key_discoveries.stalld_working_correctly: stalld detects and boosts test01's blockee, but timing is unpredictable
key_discoveries.kworker_filter_mystery_solved: The '-i kworker' filter was NOT broken - it was eliminating the starvation scenario entirely

priority_algorithm_behavior.logic: Selects task with earliest 'since' timestamp (longest starvation)
priority_algorithm_behavior.code: if (cpu_info->since > task->since) { update to earlier task }
priority_algorithm_behavior.implication: Pre-existing starving kworkers have earlier timestamps than test01's blockee
priority_algorithm_behavior.result: Kworkers get boosted repeatedly while test01 waits its turn

with_filter: Kworkers are ignored by stalld (not boosted)
Without kworker competition, test01's blockee gets CPU naturally
test01 completes in <5s WITHOUT needing stalld's intervention
No starvation occurs, so no stalld logs (test01 never reaches 5s threshold)
without_filter: Kworkers and test01 both starve and compete for boosting
test01's blockee eventually boosted but total time exceeds timeout
Exit code 137 = SIGKILL from timeout, not test01 failure

single_threaded_mode.behavior: One thread monitors all CPUs, uses cpu_starving_vector
single_threaded_mode.limitation: Can only track one starving task per CPU at a time
single_threaded_mode.selection: Always picks task with earliest starvation timestamp

observed_behavior.interpretation: 2 starving tasks (kworker + test01 blockee) but only 1 boosted
observed_behavior.boost_pattern: Repeatedly boosted every 5s for 35+ seconds before cycle restarts
observed_behavior.test01_blockee: Second starving task, waiting for kworker to finish

solution_implemented.approach: Option 2 - Increase timeout without kworker filter
rationale: Actually tests stalld's starvation detection and boosting (unlike filter approach)
Accommodates timing variability on loaded systems
Aligns with user's expectation of loaded system testing
Sets foundation for future multi-blockee starvation tests

documentation_added: Explanation of SCHED_FIFO blocker vs SCHED_OTHER blockee scenario
Description of kworker competition on loaded systems
Detailed explanation of priority algorithm (earliest timestamp wins)
Why pre-existing kworkers get boosted first
Future improvement suggestions (adaptive mode, kworker filter)

solution_implemented.commit: 0cb5e0a8c0b3 - tests: Increase test01 timeout and document kworker competition

option_1_kworker_filter.cons: Doesn't actually test stalld's boosting in many cases (test01 never starves)
option_1_kworker_filter.status: Rejected for now, documented as future improvement

option_3_disable_idle_detection.purpose: Force stalld to always parse run queues
option_3_disable_idle_detection.status: Not needed - parsing wasn't the issue

future_considerations.adaptive_mode: Use -M flag to spawn per-CPU threads for better precision
future_considerations.aggressive_mode: Use -A flag for dedicated per-CPU threads
future_considerations.multi_blockee_test: Create test with multiple blockees for more robust starvation scenarios

changes: Explained timing variability and priority algorithm
Added inline comment for timeout value (lines 35-39)

test_status.test01_wrapper: Should now pass on loaded systems (180s timeout)

expected_behavior.light_load: test01 completes quickly (<30s), kworkers may or may not appear
expected_behavior.heavy_load: Kworkers boosted repeatedly, test01 eventually boosted, completes within 180s

test_status.validation_pending: Need to run test on loaded system to verify 180s is sufficient

stalld_ignore_filter.return_value: Returns 0 when task SHOULD be ignored, -EINVAL when not ignored
stalld_ignore_filter.effect: Skips boosting but does NOT skip parsing or detection
stalld_ignore_filter.log_message: Logs 'Ignoring the thread X from consideration for boosting'

threading_modes.adaptive: Spawns per-CPU threads when needed (-M flag)
threading_modes.aggressive: Dedicated per-CPU threads from start (-A flag)
threading_modes.power_mode: Single-threaded alias (-O flag), only works with SCHED_DEADLINE

task_selection_algorithm.overload_detection: Sets overloaded=1 when multiple tasks starving on same CPU
task_selection_algorithm.limitation: Single-threaded mode can only boost one task per CPU per cycle

next_session_priorities.task: Verify test01_wrapper.sh passes on loaded system
next_session_priorities.details: Run test on system with competing load to confirm 180s timeout is sufficient

next_session_priorities.rationale: User mentioned may need this for loaded system testing
next_session_priorities.approach: Create test with multiple SCHED_OTHER blockees competing, more robust than single-blockee

next_session_priorities.details: Resume Phase 4 (Advanced Features) tests or other pending work

environment.dl_server: Directory exists, state managed by test_helpers.sh
environment.observed_kworkers: kworker/23:1H (PID 357) frequently starving on loaded system

debugging_approach: Don't assume a feature is broken - verify behavior first
Test filters in isolation to understand their actual effect
Check both code logic AND runtime behavior before concluding root cause
test_design: Single-threaded starvation tests are sensitive to competing load
Timeout values must account for worst-case priority algorithm behavior
Documentation is critical for explaining non-obvious timing behavior
stalld_internals: Single-threaded mode has inherent limitations with multiple starving tasks
Priority algorithm is fair (earliest timestamp) but can delay newer tasks
Ignore filters affect boosting, not detection/parsing
